source: User requested research and work on using Git, worktrees, push metadata, and differentiated artifact handling to keep CodeWiki hot state lean; user clarified implementation build is the publication payload, not a new capsule.
summary: Accepted Git-backed archival GC direction without new capsule abstraction.

diff_table.current_state: Closed tasks, builds, validation, and evidence can accumulate in CodeWiki graph state even when current work only needs hot active context.
diff_table.desired_state: Use Git as the cold immutable ledger and keep CodeWiki focused on hot active work, blockers, and compact ledgers.
diff_table.rationale: Graph and state should stay efficient over time while still allowing task/sprint recovery from Git.

diff_table.current_state: Publication metadata exists in implementation builds, but Git archival/push expectations are not yet explicit in system knowledge.
diff_table.desired_state: Treat the implementation build publication payload as the disciplined push package: closure evidence, checks, trailers, archive refs, digests, restore pointers, and push safety status.
diff_table.rationale: No new lexicon or artifact is needed; implementation builds already own implementation evidence and publication readiness.

diff_table.current_state: Git mechanisms for notes, custom refs, bundles, worktrees, sparse checkout, trailers, hooks, LFS/attributes, and maintenance are not captured in CodeWiki planning.
diff_table.desired_state: Document a source-backed Git-backed archival strategy and create executable implementation work for push/restore/GC behavior.
diff_table.rationale: The design must make Git serve CodeWiki needs rather than forcing CodeWiki to retain all cold artifacts in hot graph state.

diff_table.current_state: Security/privacy rules for pushing CodeWiki evidence to remotes are implicit.
diff_table.desired_state: Publication validation must require secret scanning, remote visibility checks, and policy decisions for fail/block/private evidence before any Git or remote push.
diff_table.rationale: Git history and GitHub remotes are durable; unsafe evidence cannot be casually published.

accepted_decisions.summary: Git is the cold immutable ledger; CodeWiki is the hot working set and generated routing surface.
Implementation build remains the publication/push payload; do not introduce a separate capsule abstraction.
Use custom refs and atomic push as preferred archival transport; use commit trailers as compact discoverable summaries; use worktrees/sparse checkout for restore/refinement.
Fail/block/private evidence requires explicit publication safety policy before remote push.

assumptions: Current work should avoid overlapping TASK-067 code scopes while another session holds an application/tests/build claim.
non_goals: No new artifact name such as capsule.
No immediate remote push or irreversible history rewrite.
No treating Git namespaces as security boundaries.

knowledge: Document Git-backed archival GC and implementation-build publication payload semantics.
roadmap: Create implementation task for Git-backed archival GC and restore/push contract.
code: Future task will add publication fields, push/restore helpers, GC gates, and tests after overlapping code claim clears.

knowledge: Document Git-backed archival GC and implementation-build publication payload semantics.
roadmap: Create implementation task for Git-backed archival GC and restore/push contract.
code: Future task will add publication fields, push/restore helpers, GC gates, and tests after overlapping code claim clears.
